metadata.title: Savoury Oat Porridge with Ginger-Garlic Crumbs
metadata.description: This porridge gets a savoury twist, thanks to the addition of an umami-rich soy butter and crispy ginger-garlic yumbits.
notes: Breakfasts are a solo affair at the OTK, with each person left to their own devices.
Feel free to quadruple the amount of said yumbits: they’re so good sprinkled on to eggs, rice and every other food group.
Make it your own - Skip the charred spring onions and just add some fresh herbs.
Bulk out your porridge by topping with leftover cooked veggies.
Make it gluten-free by swapping out the soy with tamari or coconut aminos.
metadata.author: Ottolenghi

ingredients.state: peeled and finely grated ginger, fresh

steps.action: Toss the spring onions in a bowl with olive oil and seasonings.

output.description: Spring onions tossed with olive oil, salt, and pepper.

steps.action: Cook the spring onions in a hot pan until softened and charred.

output.description: Spring onions cooked until softened and charred.

steps.action: Cook the ginger and garlic in the pan until crispy.

output.description: Ginger and garlic cooked until deeply golden and crispy.

ingredients.state: unsalted butter, fridge cold and cut into cubes

output.description: Eggs boiled until soft and then peeled.

steps.action: Cook the oats with water and seasonings.

output.description: Oats cooked to a loose porridge consistency.
Soy sauce and butter whisked together to create a smooth mixture.
Porridge topped with soy butter, charred spring onions, and soft-boiled eggs.
